summary: Local-analytics readers must query the resource names sync actually writes (cycle, recovery, activity=sleeps, activity-workout) and timestamp extraction must fall back to created_at, because recovery records carry no start field.
reason: The novel analytics shipped reading cycle_recovery and sleep — store names sync never writes — so sleep-debt, strain-budget, digest, trend, correlate and classify saw zero rows on every synced mirror and emitted recovery 0 / observations 0; classify additionally read sleeps (activity) where it expected workouts. A regen must keep reader resource names aligned with defaultSyncResources()/syncResourcePath() and keep the created_at fallback for time-windowed series.
validated_outcome: Against a freshly synced mirror: sleep-debt now reports baseline_recovery 53.6 with 15 observations and strain-budget latest_recovery 54 (yellow x0.7), where both previously returned the constant 0 (red). Regression test seeds the store under sync's exact resource names.
